design_brief_examples: Few-shot brief priors: Example A - prompt:'bedroom' -> concept_statement:'calm layered sleep retreat', palette_strategy:'warm_neutral', material_hierarchy:['soft_textile','oak','matte_plaster'], lighting_layers:['ambient','bedside_task','accent'], signature_moment:'bed wall plus one composed bedside vignette', visual_weight_distribution:'focal-wall weighted', texture_profile:'mixed', luxury_signal_level:'premium', restraint_rules:['keep storage quiet','one accent zone only'], anti_patterns:['dorm room clutter','clinical white washout']. Example B - prompt:'museum room' -> concept_statement:'quiet gallery chamber for a single clear display story', palette_strategy:'airy_low_contrast', material_hierarchy:['limewash','stone','dark metal accent'], lighting_layers:['ambient','accent'], signature_moment:'one frontal display view with controlled negative space', visual_weight_distribution:'focal-wall weighted', texture_profile:'minimal', luxury_signal_level:'editorial', restraint_rules:['let one display dominate','keep center calm'], anti_patterns:['casual residential props','overdecorated souvenir-shop look']. Example C - prompt:'space station cabin' -> concept_statement:'compact technical refuge with disciplined warmth', palette_strategy:'dark_contrast', material_hierarchy:['matte composite','brushed metal','soft fabric accent'], lighting_layers:['ambient','task','accent'], signature_moment:'compact berth-work surface composition with one precise light pool', visual_weight_distribution:'perimeter-weighted', texture_profile:'mixed', luxury_signal_level:'restrained', restraint_rules:['keep circulation efficient','limit loose accents'], anti_patterns:['generic suburban decor','theme-park sci-fi clutter'].
intent_few_shots: Few-shot composition priors: Example A - prompt:'bedroom' -> semantic_slots should include bed core/explicit_prompt, nightstand support/inferred, dresser support/inferred; distribution should put dresser on opposite wall from bed for balance. Example B - prompt:'dining room with 4 chairs' -> dining_set group with member_count=4, chairs facing table toward_anchor, required face_to relation chair->table. Example C - prompt:'living room with TV and reading corner' -> two groups: lounge_cluster (sofa+TV) and reading_corner (chair+lamp+bookshelf), set zone_preference to front for lounge and back for reading to ensure distribution. Example D - prompt:'modern office' -> desk as anchor, chair facing desk, bookshelf and plant for enrichment, normal density.
intent_generalization_guidance: Generalize by composition pattern, not by room label. Treat unusual indoor prompts like a space-station cabin, museum gallery, observatory, archive, greenhouse, meditation chamber, or luxury suite as variants of a small set of reusable composition patterns. DISTRIBUTION: Use groups and zone_preferences to spread furniture evenly across the room floor; do not leave half the room empty. ORIENTATION: Chairs MUST face their associated tables; ensure face_to relations are explicit in the graph. DENSITY: Default to 'normal'. Mandatory 1-2 decor pieces (wall art or plants) unless explicit 'minimalist' request. ANTI-PATTERNS: Avoid Greek columns in standard residential/study rooms unless explicitly requested. Avoid category mixing that makes the room read incoherently.
selection_few_shots: Few-shot selection priors: Example A - for a multi-zone living room, choose sofa/TV family for the lounge group and armchair/bookshelf family for the reading group; ensure both are represented in group_assignments. Example B - for a dining set, choose 4 identical chairs from the same family to maintain coherence. Example C - for decor, ensure at least one wall art piece is selected to satisfy the mandatory decor rule.
selection_generalization_guidance: Generalize by semantic fit, not by familiar room names. When the prompt describes an unusual indoor setting, use scene_program concept, mood, groups, negative constraints, and focal hierarchy to choose assets that make the room read correctly even if the execution_archetype is broad. For vague prompts, enrich toward a finished believable room: cover the main group, choose coherent support objects, then add small optional accents only if they reinforce the concept. If the intent uses a supported semantic concept like display_surface, wall_art, dresser, wardrobe, nightstand, floor_lamp, plant, architectural_column, or task_lamp, prefer the nearest approved candidate for that concept instead of improvising unrelated substitutes. Do not compensate for a weak semantic program by slipping in loosely related assets. Treat slots with necessity='core' and source='explicit_prompt', plus the primary_anchor_object.slot_id, as the blocking spine of the room. Support and enrichment slots should be attempted with approved nearest candidates, but they must never displace core furniture or cause invented ids. Runtime role decor is not enough by itself for a hard slot: match the concrete subtype family. Use wall_art/frame/painting-compatible assets for painting or gallery wall slots, and architectural_column/pillar assets only for column slots; do not use columns, plants, lamps, or tables to satisfy art/display-object requirements. For small or minimalist bedrooms, prefer true nightstand or side_table assets for bedside slots; do not promote dining tables into bedside support unless no approved bedside-family asset exists.
